title: The Devils Hands Are Idle Playthings

jokes.text: I choose to not understand these signs.
jokes.setup: Character confronted with presumably obvious warning signs
jokes.punchline: Deliberately choosing ignorance as a strategy
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor that captures Fry's willful obliviousness. Economical phrasing with mild quotability.

jokes.text: Bender! I was just not playing the holophonor.
jokes.setup: Bender catching Fry in the act
jokes.punchline: Fry's awkward attempt to deny what he was obviously doing
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke that's functional but not particularly memorable. Fits Fry's character.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, you should try not stinking at it.
jokes.punchline: Bender's brutal honesty about Fry's skill level
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender insult with good economy. Character-appropriate cruelty.

jokes.text: Isn't it time you gave up all hope of ever improving yourself in any way?
jokes.punchline: Bender suggests complete surrender to mediocrity
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of motivational friend trope. Perfect Bender nihilism with strong quotability.

jokes.text: You know, sometimes I wish your real parents were still alive. Not often, though.
jokes.setup: Seemingly sentimental moment about Fry's parents
jokes.punchline: Immediate cruel qualification
jokes.explanation: Masterful dark comedy timing. The pause and qualification make it brutally funny. Classic Bender.

jokes.text: Is it true that you're a robot? I prefer the term 'love machine.'
jokes.setup: Polite elderly woman asking about robot status
jokes.punchline: Bender's inappropriately sexual self-description
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's inappropriate responses to polite society.

jokes.text: Your kid is great. How hard you say you had to hit him? Fairly hard.
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of child abuse
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that works through matter-of-fact delivery of inappropriate content.

jokes.text: It's too grumpy!
jokes.punchline: Audience member's specific complaint about a snail's emotional state
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity makes this memorable. The complaint about a snail's mood is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: You know what always cheers me up? Laughing at other people's misfortunes!
jokes.punchline: Bender's twisted version of encouragement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender philosophy. Takes a supportive friend moment and makes it cruel but honest.

jokes.text: I used to listen for hours while he sat naked on my couch and improvised.
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortably specific detail about nudity
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor through oversharing. The casual mention of nudity makes it awkward.

jokes.text: So musicians really Rodger your Hammerstein?
jokes.punchline: Sexual pun using musical theater composers' names
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels. Creative use of Rodgers & Hammerstein as euphemism.

jokes.text: He was pasty and hunched. Pretty boy.
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic 'pretty boy' after listing ugly traits
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective ironic contrast. Good timing and economy.

jokes.text: Then one day I found someone else's couch fibers on his butt.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous forensic evidence of cheating
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd way to discover infidelity. The specificity of 'couch fibers' makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: And by devil, I mean Robot Devil. And by metaphorically, I mean get your coat.
jokes.punchline: Clarifying it's literal and immediate
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure. Each clarification makes it more literal and immediate. Great writing technique.

jokes.text: You hit a sour note about 200 years back, Doug!
jokes.punchline: Comically long time span for holding a grudge about one note
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor playing with immortal characters' perspective on time. Robot Devil's pettiness works.

jokes.text: Hell is full of 10-Year-Olds who wanted exactly the same thing.
jokes.punchline: Implication that this desire literally damns children
jokes.explanation: Dark but clever commentary on entitlement. Good world-building through humor.

jokes.text: With hands like that you'll be lucky to master a belt buckle.
jokes.punchline: Suggesting he can't handle the simplest fastening task
jokes.explanation: Solid insult with good specificity. Belt buckle is a perfect example of basic coordination.

jokes.text: Sure would. Oh, well, goodbye.
jokes.punchline: Fry completely missing the obvious opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry obliviousness. His casual dismissal of magical help is beautifully in character.

jokes.text: I think he's willing to make some kind of deal with the devil with you.
jokes.punchline: Bender stating the completely obvious as revelation
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man moment for Bender, having to explain the obvious to Fry.

jokes.text: It's just the sort of guy I am.
jokes.punchline: Presenting it as casual personality trait
jokes.explanation: Good understatement humor. Robot Devil's casual attitude toward evil is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: If you don't take this offer, I will lose all respect for you and punch you.
jokes.setup: Trying to convince Fry to make deal
jokes.punchline: Threat of physical violence for moral choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - emotional manipulation followed by physical threat. Great escalation.

jokes.text: You sure I won't know him? Definitely probably not.
jokes.punchline: Deliberately vague and contradictory answer
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that signals the devil's deception. 'Definitely probably not' is perfectly evasive.

jokes.text: I got 100 bucks on Rectal Exam-Bot!
jokes.punchline: Betting on robot with unfortunate medical specialty
jokes.explanation: Good background gag. The name is memorable and appropriately ridiculous for Futurama.

jokes.text: I got 100 bucks on Rectal Exam-Bot!
jokes.setup: Wheel of robots spinning to determine victim
jokes.punchline: Someone betting on a robot with an unfortunate name/function
jokes.explanation: Background gag with mildly crude robot name. Simple but effective world-building humor.

jokes.text: It's not ironic, it's just coincidental.
jokes.punchline: Correct grammar/usage distinction
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor addressing common language misuse. Smart and economical, though limited impact.

jokes.text: Oh, sorry. That'll wear off in a couple of days.
jokes.setup: Fry's confident declaration about winning Leela
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's just temporary magical confidence
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Fry's moment. Reveals the artificial nature of his confidence boost.

jokes.text: These things are always touching me in places.
jokes.setup: Robot Devil complaining about Fry's hands
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate touching by his own replacement hands
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy with uncomfortable implications. Moderately effective.

jokes.text: Yeah, they get around. But I'm afraid we had a deal.
jokes.setup: Robot Devil complaining about wandering hands
jokes.punchline: Fry's matter-of-fact acknowledgment with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Mild innuendo about his hands' behavior. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Oh, Zoidberg, at last you're becoming a crafty consumer.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg falling for obvious sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Praising himself for being manipulated
jokes.explanation: Good Zoidberg characterization. His misunderstanding of 'crafty consumer' fits perfectly.

jokes.text: Your latest performance was as delectable as dipping my bottom over and over into a bath of the silkiest oils and creams.
jokes.punchline: Extremely sensual and inappropriate comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hedonism-Bot dialogue. Character-specific over-the-top sensuality works well.

jokes.text: You are the sole diversion in what has been a pale and unamusing season.
jokes.punchline: Inadvertent commentary on disappointing social season
jokes.explanation: Good character voice for Hedonism-Bot. The aristocratic language and complaints work.

jokes.text: A man writing an opera about a woman? Oh, sirrah. How deliciously absurd.
jokes.setup: Fry wanting to write opera about Leela
jokes.punchline: Treating normal concept as scandalous novelty
jokes.explanation: Good character humor playing with aristocratic sensibilities. 'Sirrah' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: So is a peacock, but you don't eat it until it's cooked.
jokes.punchline: Completely illogical cooking metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly nonsensical Fry logic. The metaphor makes no sense but sounds profound.

jokes.text: I've been a fool. A fully justified, prudent fool.
jokes.setup: Leela admitting she was wrong about Fry
jokes.punchline: Qualifying her foolishness as reasonable
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's logical nature even in emotional admission.

jokes.text: Ah! They're so cold! And yet hell is so hot!
jokes.punchline: Temperature contradiction between body parts and environment
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy playing with the hand swap consequences.

jokes.text: I suppose I've always wondered what it would be like to be more annoying.
jokes.setup: Robot Devil asking what Bender wants
jokes.punchline: Bender wanting to enhance his worst quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender self-awareness. He knows exactly what he is and wants more of it.

jokes.text: Grabby and Squeezy? Never! I love these guys!
jokes.setup: Robot Devil asking for Bender's hands
jokes.punchline: Bender's emotional attachment to his hands with cute names
jokes.explanation: Adorable character moment. Bender naming his hands shows unexpected sentimentality.

jokes.text: My ridiculously circuitous plan is one quarter complete.
jokes.punchline: Villain acknowledging his own over-complicated plotting
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor about villain tropes. Self-awareness about unnecessarily complex plans is clever.

jokes.text: Pretty annoying, huh, Leela? What? Are you talking? Oh, God, I'm deaf!
jokes.setup: Bender using air horn to annoy Leela
jokes.punchline: Accidentally deafening her completely
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mild annoyance to serious consequence. Sets up later plot point.

jokes.text: Oh, how delightfully ironic.
jokes.setup: Leela can't hear the opera she inspired
jokes.punchline: Robot Devil's use of 'ironic' (callback to earlier correction)
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier grammar correction. Creates continuity and character consistency.

jokes.text: It's not ironic. It's just mean.
jokes.punchline: Leela making similar correction to Fry's earlier one
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure. Leela now making the same grammar correction reinforces the running gag.

jokes.text: I watch TV with that guy!
jokes.punchline: Someone's minimal connection being their entire relationship
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about weak social connections and false intimacy.

jokes.text: Amy, Fry's looking at me. What am I supposed to be feeling?
jokes.setup: Leela can't hear the opera but sees Fry looking at her
jokes.punchline: Asking for emotional cues since she can't hear the music
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny moment showing Leela's predicament. Her practical approach to emotional situations is very in-character.

jokes.text: I don't recall ever fighting Godzilla. But that is so what I would've done.
jokes.punchline: Leela's confident assessment of her hypothetical actions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leela characterization. Shows her confidence and action-hero mindset beautifully.

jokes.text: I'll just have a Shirley Hemple.
jokes.punchline: Robot Devil's version of 'Shirley Temple' with hemlock reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with poison reference appropriate for the devil character.

jokes.text: I can hear like a safecracker!
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison to criminal profession requiring keen hearing
jokes.explanation: Good specific comparison for enhanced hearing. Shows Leela's practical mindset and slight criminal awareness.

jokes.text: Stupider? This opera's as lousy as it is brilliant!
jokes.punchline: Hedonism-Bot's contradictory assessment
jokes.explanation: Good paradoxical criticism that captures the opera's mixed quality perfectly.

jokes.text: Your lyrics lack subtlety. You can't just have your characters announce how they feel.
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on bad musical theater writing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor about musical theater conventions. Widely quotable and applicable beyond the show.

jokes.text: That makes me feel angry!
jokes.punchline: Immediately does exactly what he criticized
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate subversion of his own criticism. The hypocrisy is instant and hilarious.

jokes.text: Now that is irony...
jokes.punchline: Robot Devil finally using 'irony' correctly
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the running grammar gag. Robot Devil finally gets it right.

jokes.text: 'I'll give you my hand.' In marriage... 'The use of words expressing something Other than their literal intention'... Now that is irony...
jokes.punchline: Defining irony while using a pun, then claiming this IS actually irony
jokes.explanation: Clever resolution of the irony running gag - this actually IS ironic. The Robot Devil finally gets it right while being deviously clever.

jokes.text: I can't believe the devil... Is so unforgiving... I can't believe... Everybody's just ad-libbing...
jokes.punchline: Bender breaking the fourth wall about the improvised nature
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-joke about the characters making up the opera as they go. Bender's awareness of the theatrical situation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Surgery in an opera? How wonderfully decadent! And just as I was beginning to lose interest.
jokes.setup: Robot Devil performing hand surgery on stage
jokes.punchline: Hedonism-Bot's excitement about the extreme nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hedonism-Bot reaction - only interested when things become truly extreme and transgressive.

jokes.text: My hands! My horrible human hands!
jokes.setup: Fry getting his original hands back
jokes.punchline: Treating return to normal as horror
jokes.explanation: Good reversal showing Fry's attachment to his magical abilities. Natural character reaction.

jokes.text: And what did you do to my nails? I cleaned them.
jokes.punchline: Basic hygiene presented as violation
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Fry's poor hygiene habits. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: The music's bad and you should feel bad!
jokes.setup: Critical reaction to Fry's reduced musical ability
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's harsh but honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Became a widely-used internet meme. Perfect economy and Zoidberg's blunt honesty. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: The music's bad and you should feel bad!
jokes.setup: Fry's terrible playing with his original hands
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's brutal but honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Iconic Zoidberg line that became a meme. Perfect structure, devastating honesty, and completely in character. This is peak comedy writing.

jokes.text: Greatest opera of all time sucks!
jokes.punchline: Contradictory headline combining praise and criticism
jokes.explanation: Good paradoxical headline that captures the opera's mixed reception effectively.

tmdb_overview: Fry makes a deal with the Robot Devil so he can become a skillful musician and win Leela's heart.
